svg.aria-label: Animated diagram: a value record is appended into a 64 MB page at free_offset while an 8-byte slot entry is written at the page end, so records are addressed by segment id rather than byte offset; when the two regions meet, a new page opens and segment ids restart.
svg.text: Value log: a page and its slot table
Records append forward from the header; the slot table grows backward from the page end. A pointer is (page_offset, segment_id).
ValuePointer { bucket: 3, page_offset: 0, segment_id: 1 } → packed into one u128
ValuePointer { bucket: 3, page_offset: 0, segment_id: 1 } → packed into one u128
ValuePointer { bucket: 3, page_offset: 0, segment_id: 2 } → packed into one u128
ValuePointer { bucket: 3, page_offset: 0, segment_id: 3 } → packed into one u128
ValuePointer { bucket: 3, page_offset: 0, segment_id: 2 } → packed into one u128
ValuePointer { bucket: 3, page_offset: 0, segment_id: 3 } → packed into one u128
ValuePointer { bucket: 3, page_offset: 0, segment_id: 4 } → packed into one u128
ValuePointer { bucket: 3, page_offset: 64 MB, segment_id: 1 } → packed into one u128
ValuePointer { bucket: 3, page_offset: 64 MB, segment_id: 2 } → packed into one u128

g.text: A fresh 64 MB page. Its 32-byte header holds free_offset (32), table_offset (page end) and next_segment_id (1).
write_record(v1): the record is appended at free_offset, and an 8-byte slot entry (segment 1 → record offset) is written at the page's end.
Each append grows the record region up and the slot table down. free_offset and table_offset march toward each other.
Writes are append-only — nothing already written is ever moved or rewritten in place.
Read of pointer (page 0, segment 2): slot 2 is at page_end − 2×8. It yields the record's offset, and the record is read there.
Keys address records by segment id, never by byte offset — so GC can move a record's bytes and only its slot entry has to agree.
The next record plus its slot no longer fit between free_offset and table_offset → this page is full.
A new page opens at the file's tail. Segment ids restart at 1 — the pointer's page_offset is what keeps them unambiguous.
The LSM stores all of this as one u128: bucket (32b) | page_offset (64b) | segment_id (32b).

svg.text: BYTES USED IN THE CURRENT PAGE
Illustrative record size (108 B). The 32 B page header is not charted.
